value.flow: Events are captured locally in SQLite and synced to the backend in batches when connectivity is available.

analytics.webreader.events.summary: WebReader tracks search, reading, chapter, download, scroll, and TTS behavior through local events that later sync to analytics.

api.webreader.rest.summary: The FastAPI REST surface covers source discovery, novel search, novel metadata, chapters, event ingestion, and analytics reporting.

value.constraint: all training decisions are tuned around low VRAM stability

arch.consumer-gpu-training-profile.summary: HexTTs is intentionally tuned for stable VITS training on an RTX 3050 Ti class consumer GPU with limited VRAM.

arch.hextts.v053-flags.summary: v0.5.3 baked architecture flags into checkpoints (pre-norm, GELU, sigmoid decoder, 3x1+GroupNorm posterior, extra duration context conv, MLP prior projection).

interactions.gui: Provides state and events for display.

value.role: Single point of coordination across adjacent ECG Digital Twin layers.

arch.layer-interactions.summary: The system layer acts as the coordination point between core ECG outputs, signal metrics, alarm logic, GUI state, and safety verification.

arch.mode-management.summary: Mode management defines NORMAL, DEGRADED, and FAIL-SAFE behavior and transitions when uncertainty or unsafe runtime conditions occur.

value.purpose: Load and validate system parameters, reject invalid scenarios, and initialize runtime state before monitoring begins.

arch.system-layer.configuration.summary: System initialization depends on validated configuration and scenario checks before any monitoring pipeline is allowed to run.

value.safetyGoal: Unsafe signal conditions should not propagate silently to the user interface.

arch.system-layer.responsibilities.summary: The system layer is responsible for converting low-level ECG processing into deterministic, supervised, and safety-controlled monitoring behavior.

value.eventQueue: Events are written to SQLite sync_queue when offline.
syncBehavior: Batch sync events to POST /events/batch when online
Optionally refresh metadata for previously viewed novels

arch.webreader.offline.sync.summary: WebReader's offline-first model stores reading content and progress locally while queueing analytics events for later batch sync.

value.dashboard: Lightweight React dashboard or minimal HTML served by backend

arch.webreader.stack.summary: The required WebReader stack combines React Native TypeScript, on-device SQLite, FastAPI server-side scraping, Python analytics, and a lightweight dashboard.

backend.webreader.scraping.policy.summary: Server-side scraping must be allowlisted, rate-limited, cached, resilient to layout changes, and controlled with a source kill-switch.

backend.webreader.source.adapter.summary: Each whitelisted website should be encapsulated as a source adapter that normalizes search, metadata, chapter lists, and chapter text.

computedMetrics: TTS usage share versus normal reading

data.webreader.backend.analytics.summary: Backend analytics stores raw synced events and computes daily or weekly engagement, popularity, completion, drop-off, and TTS usage metrics.

value.purpose: Local offline cache for novel metadata, chapter content, downloads, reading state, bookmarks, raw events, and pending sync state.

data.webreader.mobile.sqlite.summary: The mobile SQLite schema is the local source of truth for offline reading, progress, bookmarks, downloads, and queued analytics events.

decision.css-single-source-of-truth.value: Refactored public/css/styles.css from 1018 to 908 lines. Removed the duplicate override block at the bottom that redefined about 25 selectors with CSS variables. Moved :root tokens to the top. Now every selector has exactly one definition that uses var(--token). Added new tokens: --border-soft, --text-faint, --accent-hover, --danger. Visual output preserved (override values were the effective state). Adding new styles with vars now works for palette switching.
decision.css-single-source-of-truth.summary: CSS collapsed 2 duplicate blocks into one var-based source of truth (-110 lines)

decision.dashboard-non-overlap.value: Automatic dashboard graph layouts must prevent node overlap. Collision prevention applies to default radial, hierarchical, force, and clicked radial-focus layouts. The resolver should use actual visual node boxes, including circular degree-scaled collapsed nodes and expanded rectangular cards, with spacing margin for hover/focus growth.
decision.dashboard-non-overlap.summary: Automatic dashboard layouts must resolve node collisions using actual visual boxes.

decision.dashboard-stability-first.value: Dashboard improvement priority favored reliability before deeper UI refactors: harden realtime WebSocket behavior with reconnect/request timeout; validate startup modules; make edge labels obey settings; protect force layout performance; guard app init; and validate import files before server validation.
decision.dashboard-stability-first.summary: Dashboard reliability work prioritized realtime, startup, edge labels, layout cache, and import guards.

decision.dashboard.command-palette.value: Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) opens a centered command-palette overlay (#palette in public/index.html). Filter is case-insensitive across key + summary; results sorted by importance desc. Up/Down navigates, Enter calls openDetail(key, currentEntries[key]) reusing the existing focus+pan+zoom pipeline, Escape closes. Click outside the palette card also closes. Markup is hidden by default via the [hidden] attribute.
decision.dashboard.command-palette.summary: Ctrl+K palette: fuzzy match on key+summary, importance-sorted, Enter focuses the node.

decision.dashboard.detail-panel-polish.value: Two detail panel improvements: (1) Copy button added to header — copies entry value (JSON pretty-printed if object) to clipboard with 1.2s Copied/Failed feedback. (2) .dp-value changed from max-height:210px to flex:1 with min-height:140px so it fills available panel height instead of leaving empty space below the metadata rows. .dp-body is now a flex column.
decision.dashboard.detail-panel-polish.summary: Detail panel: copy button added, value box now flex-fills panel height instead of fixed 210px cap

decision.dashboard.first-load-fit.value: On first load only, the dashboard auto-fits to the highest-(importance + sqrt(degree)*1.5) node's depth-1 neighborhood instead of the full graph. fitToTopCluster() in dashboard.js runs after the initial renderGraph; subsequent loads keep the user's view. Skipped if entries.length < 8 or cluster < 3 (graph too small to need framing).
decision.dashboard.first-load-fit.summary: First-load view frames the most important node's neighborhood, not the whole graph.

decision.dashboard.force-engine.value: The 'force' layout mode now uses d3-force (loaded from CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7/dist/d3.min.js) instead of the previous custom 220-iteration O(n²) Verlet simulation. computeForceLayout in public/js/dashboard.js builds nodes/links and runs d3.forceSimulation with charge(-450), link(distance 140, strength 0.5), forceCenter(0,0), forceCollide(70), and a custom clusterForce(0.18) that pulls each node toward its namespace centroid. Runs 300 ticks synchronously then stops. Falls back to seeded positions if d3 fails to load.
decision.dashboard.force-engine.summary: Force layout swapped to d3-force quadtree-backed sim with custom namespace clusterForce.

decision.dashboard.label-counter-scale.value: Node labels stay readable at low zoom via CSS clamp counter-scaling. applyTransform() in dashboard.js sets `--scene-scale: ${scale}` on #viewport. .node-mini-title uses `font-size: clamp(12px, calc(12px / var(--scene-scale, 1)), 22px)` so when scene-scale is 0.5 the label renders at ~22px (capped), keeping pixel size constant on screen across zoom levels. No JS-side label swap needed.
decision.dashboard.label-counter-scale.summary: Pure-CSS clamp + --scene-scale variable keeps node labels readable at low zoom (caps at 22px).

decision.dashboard.layout-algorithms.value: Implemented 3 layout modes in pure JS (no d3 dependency needed): radial = namespace-sector layout with importance-based ring placement (high importance inner), force = 300-iteration Verlet simulation with O(n2) repulsion + spring edges + center gravity + 0.82 damping, hierarchical = existing dagre LR layout. computeLayout dispatches on graphSettings.layoutMode. Switching mode triggers full re-render + fit.
decision.dashboard.layout-algorithms.summary: 3 layout algorithms (radial/force/hierarchical) implemented in pure JS, dispatched from computeLayout

decision.dashboard.layout-modes.value: The graph dashboard supports three layout algorithms selectable via Settings → Layout → Algorithm: 'radial' (default; namespace sectors with importance pulling nodes toward center), 'force' (custom velocity Verlet, 220 iters, repulsion+spring+cluster centroid pull), and 'hierarchical' (dagre LR with namespace compound clusters via setParent). computeLayout() in public/js/dashboard.js dispatches based on graphSettings.layoutMode. Rationale: a single dagre layout produced a hairball; offering three modes lets users pick the structure that fits their inspection task.
decision.dashboard.layout-modes.summary: Three layout algorithms (radial/force/hierarchical) selectable via settings; computeLayout dispatches on layoutMode.

decision.dashboard.namespace-visual-enco.value: Visual encoding to break the hairball: (1) node hue derived from a stable hash of the namespace prefix (key.split('.')[0]) at HSL 92% saturation, 64% lightness — high saturation is intentional for cluster legibility; (2) node size driven by importance + sqrt(degree)*0.5 scaled by graphSettings.nodeScale; (3) edges dimmed via .edge-group opacity 0.25 with .highlight bumping to 1 + glow filter; (4) translucent rounded SVG rect hulls (hsla(hue,80%,55%,0.07) fill, dashed stroke) drawn behind each namespace cluster with a label, pointer-events:none, singletons skipped.
decision.dashboard.namespace-visual-enco.summary: Hairball broken by namespace-hue coloring (HSL 92/64), importance sizing, dim edges (0.25), and cluster hull rects.

decision.dashboard.peek-strip.value: Chose persistent bottom-left peek strip over hover tooltips. Fixed at left:60px bottom:16px. Shows namespace dot, key, importance bar, tags, summary. Wired to mouseenter/mouseleave AND focus/blur.
decision.dashboard.peek-strip.summary: Peek strip at bottom-left shows node key/importance/tags/summary on hover and keyboard focus

decision.dashboard.settings-redesign.value: Full settings panel CSS redesign: section headers now have 2px accent left-edge bar when expanded, filled triangular chevrons (down/right), 10px uppercase monospace titles. Range sliders: custom track + glowing accent-2 thumb that scales on hover. Toggles: CSS-only pill using appearance:none + ::after pseudo sliding thumb. Relation chips: hidden checkbox, whole chip toggles with accent glow. Palette grid: 3-column, radio hidden, selected card glows. Value readouts: accent-2 green + bold. Profile row buttons: accent hover glow.
decision.dashboard.settings-redesign.summary: Settings panel redesigned: pill toggles, glowing sliders, accent section bars, chip filters, palette grid

value.reason: 0.5 prevented the duration predictor from learning reliably

decision.duration-predictor-dropout.summary: Duration predictor dropout is locked at 0.3 because the prior value of 0.5 blocked reliable learning of duration targets.

decision.hextts-timeline-source.value: Decision: timeline data for index.html is sourced from docs/timeline-data.js instead of embedding data directly in the page. docs/timeline-data.js now exposes both browser global TIMELINE_DATA and CommonJS module.exports for compatibility.
decision.hextts-timeline-source.summary: Timeline source moved to docs/timeline-data.js with browser + CommonJS export.

decision.import-merge.value: Dashboard JSON imports should add to the existing memory graph, not replace it. Public replace-mode can remain for backup/restore compatibility, but the dashboard default is merge/add mode. If uploaded memory keys already exist, skip existing entries rather than overwrite or fail; report skipped entries and duplicate edges in stats.
decision.import-merge.summary: Dashboard JSON upload adds to memory by default; existing keys are skipped, not overwritten.

decision.mcp-relate-availability.value: In current session, user indicated relate is now available for explicit graph edges. Proceeding to add explicit relation edges for HexTTs nodes.
decision.mcp-relate-availability.summary: Relate is available now; add explicit edges for HexTTs memory nodes.

decision.mcp-relate-tool.value: Added memory_relate and memory_unrelate to the stdio MCP adapter so MCP clients can edit graph edges without going through the WebSocket. Validation imports RELATION_TYPES from src/protocol.js to keep one source of truth. Files: src/mcp-tools.js (handlers + validators), mcp-server.mjs (registerTool blocks). All 7 existing MCP tests pass; no new tests yet.
decision.mcp-relate-tool.summary: MCP adapter now exposes memory_relate / memory_unrelate; relation validation shares protocol.js RELATION_TYPES

decision.portfolio-project-ctas.value: Project CTA arrows in the portfolio should be real accessible external links. Because exact project repository URLs are not currently available, all project CTAs currently link to the GitHub profile https://github.com/pruthuraj. When exact repo or demo links are available, replace the fallback per project rather than inventing URLs.
decision.portfolio-project-ctas.summary: Project arrows are accessible links; fallback target is https://github.com/pruthuraj until repo URLs are known.

decision.portfolio-public-contact.value: The portfolio should publish email, LinkedIn, and GitHub contact links, but omit the CV phone number unless the user explicitly requests adding it later. This preserves privacy while keeping recruiter contact paths available.
decision.portfolio-public-contact.summary: Public contact uses email/LinkedIn/GitHub; phone number stays private unless explicitly requested.

value.verification: Unit and safety test cases are expected to verify these properties.

decision.safety-properties.summary: The system layer defines explicit safety properties that prevent invalid execution, silent transitions, unlogged alarms, and unsafe continued operation.

value.rationale: Separate safety-critical decision logic from signal mathematics and UI concerns.

decision.separation-of-concerns.summary: The system layer intentionally separates safety-critical control decisions from signal mathematics and user interface rendering.

decision.settings-module-refactor.value: Dashboard settings refactored into public/js/settings/{schema,store,profiles,apply,panel,index}.js. Schema-driven panel: adding a setting = one entry in schema.SECTIONS + one branch in dashboard.js handleSettingsChange. New features: minImportance + per-relation-type filters (applied in filteredGraph wrapping renderGraph), node size / label size / edge thickness sliders, layoutMode picker (UI only — no rendering swap yet), named profiles + JSON export/import, search box, collapsible sections with persistence, modified-dot indicators per section. Existing setting IDs preserved (setting-zoom-speed etc) for any external code. dashboard.js shrunk 1771→1654 lines; new modules add ~853 lines. All node --check pass.
decision.settings-module-refactor.summary: Settings UI extracted to schema-driven modules under public/js/settings/; new filters, profiles, search, collapsible sections added

decision.sharedMemory.curation-tools.value: sharedMemory now ships first-class curation primitives: (1) memory_set returns a `warnings` array when summary/tags/importance are missing — write still succeeds; (2) memory_audit returns {zombies, orphans, duplicates, stale, expired, counts} where zombies = importance=0 OR no tags OR empty summary; (3) memory_bulk_set / memory_bulk_relate apply many writes in one round-trip with per-item failure isolation; (4) /status exposes audit counts (cached 5s); (5) the dashboard shows a red "! N" badge in the toolbar when zombies > 0. Implementation in src/{protocol,server,memory-store,mcp-tools}.js plus mcp-server.mjs registrations.
decision.sharedMemory.curation-tools.summary: Hygiene layer: warnings on set, memory_audit, bulk ops, /status counts, dashboard badge.

decision.sharedMemory.cytoscape-migratio.value: {"summary": "Replaced D3+dagre custom DOM graph renderer with Cytoscape.js canvas renderer. Dropped radial layout. Two layouts remain: force (cose) and hierarchical (dagre plugin via cytoscape-dagre).", "rationale": "Cytoscape.js provides built-in pan/zoom/drag, canvas rendering (no DOM node per node), class-based styling, and a clean plugin ecosystem. Eliminates ~320 lines of custom DOM/SVG edge code.", "filesChanged": ["public/index.html", "public/js/dashboard/state.js", "public/js/dashboard/layout.js", "public/js/dashboard/edges.js", "public/js/dashboard/nodes.js", "public/js/dashboard/viewport.js", "public/js/dashboard/main.js", "public/js/dashboard/graph-detail.js", "public/js/dashboard/identity.js", "public/js/dashboard/realtime.js", "public/js/dashboard/utils.js", "public/js/dashboard/settings-palette.js", "public/js/settings/schema.js", "public/css/styles.css"]}
decision.sharedMemory.cytoscape-migratio.summary: Migrated sharedMemory dashboard from D3+dagre DOM renderer to Cytoscape.js canvas renderer

decision.sharedMemory.node-card-ui.value: {"summary": "Cytoscape nodes rendered as card-style round-rectangles (168×78px) using SVG data URI background-image. Each card shows: left identity-color accent bar (4px), namespace prefix (small gray), sub-key (bold white), summary (muted), importance dots (5 circles, filled = round(imp/2)).", "rationale": "Pure SVG background-image (no plugin) re-evaluates on cy.style().update(). Avoids cytoscape-node-html-label plugin. SVG content generated by buildNodeSvg() in layout.js using encodeURIComponent.", "filesChanged": ["public/js/dashboard/layout.js"]}
decision.sharedMemory.node-card-ui.summary: Card-style Cytoscape nodes: 168×78 round-rect with SVG background showing ns/key/summary/importance dots

value.reason: keep embedding layer exactly aligned with dataset phoneme mapping

decision.vocab-size-source-of-truth.summary: The model vocabulary size is now taken directly from dataset code so the embedding layer matches the real phoneme inventory.

fact.dashboard.js-file-map.value: The dashboard JS was split from a monolithic dashboard.js into focused files: state.js (shared state + DOM refs), utils.js (helpers), layout.js (all layout algorithms), nodes.js (node creation + focus), edges.js (SVG edges), identity.js (identity panel), import.js (import panel), graph-detail.js (detail panel), viewport.js (pan/zoom), realtime.js (WebSocket + RPC), settings-palette.js (settings onChange handler), main.js (bootstrap). Load order matters — no ES modules.
fact.dashboard.js-file-map.summary: Dashboard JS split into 12 focused IIFE files loaded in order from index.html

fact.hextts-timeline-data-contract.value: Timeline UI contract for HexTTs webpage: index.html reads events/meta from docs/timeline-data.js via global TIMELINE_DATA payload. Event schema: date|null, displayDate, version|null, title, sourceFile, type(release|patch|docs). Data is rendered ascending (undated first). supports: [decision.hextts-timeline-source].
fact.hextts-timeline-data-contract.summary: HexTTs timeline contract: index.html consumes docs/timeline-data.js with stable event schema.

fact.portfolio-current-content.value: The portfolio content has been updated from placeholder designer/studio copy to CV-based content: identity Pruthuraj Parikh, Frankfurt am Main, MSc High Integrity Systems, ML engineering, backend development, data engineering, high-integrity software, Werkstudent availability, public email pruthurajparikh@gmail.com, LinkedIn/GitHub links, and CV projects HexTTs, ECG Digital Twin, Datascope, and WebReader. Phone number is intentionally omitted from the public site.
fact.portfolio-current-content.summary: Portfolio now uses CV content: Pruthuraj Parikh, MSc HIS, ML/backend/data, four CV projects.

value.purpose: Windows-native forced alignment replacement for MFA
pipeline: DataLoader with num_workers loads and resamples audio
padded batch to wav2vec2 with lengths mask
g2p_en word-level cache for phoneme distribution
value.gpu_speedup_estimate: 5x with batch_size=8 on RTX 3050 Ti (~22 min vs ~1.8h CPU)

feature.align-torchaudio.summary: scripts/align_torchaudio.py is a Windows-native MFA alternative using batched wav2vec2 forced alignment with word-level g2p caching.

value.goal: reduce OOM risk and padding waste

feature.cached-loader-hardening.summary: The cached loader was hardened to filter long samples, reduce padding waste, and provide better visibility into skipped data.

value.benefit: reduces repeated librosa and phoneme conversion overhead during every epoch

feature.cached-training-pipeline.summary: HexTTs now uses a cached training pipeline that precomputes mel features and phoneme ids to improve epoch speed and GPU utilization.

feature.clean-phoneme-metadata.summary: The dataset preparation step was patched so train and validation metadata now contain clean ARPAbet style phoneme sequences instead of raw words.

value.acknowledgement: Alarm acknowledgement is handled in a controlled and traceable manner.

feature.execution-pipeline.summary: The execution pipeline runs deterministic monitoring steps and records controlled alarm acknowledgement behavior.

feature.logging-traceability.summary: Logging and traceability capture safety-relevant events, mode transitions, and alarm activity in auditable machine-readable form.

value.purpose: Derive patient-specific alarm configuration and coordinate nurse call event lifecycle through filesystem-based signaling.

feature.nurse-call-integration.summary: Nurse call integration provides a controlled event lifecycle for alarm-related nurse call signaling and resolution.

value.purpose: Admit patients, initialize patient-specific state, support scripted CSV admission, and load PTB-XL ECG records without external WFDB binaries.

feature.patient-admission.summary: Patient admission initializes patient-specific runtime state and supports both scripted admission workflows and PTB-XL record loading.

value.purpose: improve convergence stability and speech quality

feature.training-quality-upgrades.summary: A six part training quality upgrade was added to improve convergence behavior and reduce poor speech generation during VITS training.

value.failureResponse: Force transition into a safe mode when execution becomes stale or unresponsive.
value.riskControlled: Silent failures or frozen monitoring loops.

feature.watchdog-supervision.summary: The watchdog supervises runtime responsiveness and prevents stale or frozen monitoring loops from continuing silently.

feature.webreader.reader.summary: The reader experience centers on customizable chapter reading, progress persistence, bookmarks, navigation, and offline downloads.

feature.webreader.tts.summary: WebReader TTS must use only on-device speech synthesis with playback controls and local event tracking.

value.symptom: forced_align crash: targets Tensor shouldn't contain blank index
value.root_cause: hyphen '-' is index 0 (blank) in WAV2VEC2_ASR_BASE_960H labels; hyphenated LJSpeech words like 'self-made' encoded the hyphen as token 0
value.fix: _is_content_char filter requires label_to_idx.get(ch, 0) > 0 in encode_text and get_word_frame_ranges so hyphens are silently dropped

insight.align-blank-token-hyphen.summary: WAV2VEC2 forced_align crashes when targets contain index 0; HexTTs aligner filters hyphens because '-' is the blank token in this model.

insight.claude-code-agents-no-hot-reload.value: Claude Code reads .claude/agents/*.md files only at session start. Adding or editing an agent file in an active session does NOT make it available — calls to that agent type return "Agent type X not found". Restart Claude Code (or open a fresh session) for new/changed agents to register. Same applies to user-scoped agents at ~/.claude/agents/. Workaround for testing: use general-purpose agent or run inline in the current session.
insight.claude-code-agents-no-hot-reload.summary: Claude Code loads .claude/agents/*.md only at session start — no hot reload, restart to pick up new agents

insight.dagre.synthetic-cluster-nodes.value: When using dagre compound graphs (setParent for clustering), g.nodes() returns the synthetic cluster nodes alongside real ones. In dashboard.js the convention is to prefix synthetic cluster ids with '__ns__' and filter them out when extracting positions: `if (key.startsWith('__ns__')) continue;`. Forgetting the filter leaks ghost positions into the renderer.
insight.dagre.synthetic-cluster-nodes.summary: Dagre compound graphs return synthetic cluster nodes in g.nodes(); filter '__ns__' prefix before rendering.

insight.dashboard-collision-source.value: Dashboard node overlap came from layouts placing slot centers without a final collision pass against actual rendered node boxes. This became more visible after collapsed nodes scaled by edge degree, rectangular expansion, radial focus rings, and hover growth were added.
insight.dashboard-collision-source.summary: Node overlap came from center-based layout without final visual-box collision resolution.

insight.dashboard.css-duplicate-blocks.value: The sharedMemory dashboard CSS had two conflicting settings-section rule blocks: old one (line ~1098) set padding:10px 0 on .settings-section; new one (line ~1343) overrode only border-bottom, not padding. Collapsed sections still had full padding creating dead vertical space. Also old .setting-row had a fixed 132px column for the select input causing the select value span to render alongside the dropdown. Fix: removed old block, added [data-collapsed=true]{padding-bottom:0}, and added select ~ .setting-value{display:none}.
insight.dashboard.css-duplicate-blocks.summary: Duplicate CSS settings blocks caused empty gaps between collapsed sections and double-display on selects

insight.dashboard.dead-setting.value: A setting declared in public/js/settings/schema.js is not automatically wired anywhere — the schema only renders the UI and persists the value. To make a setting actually do something, you must hook it in the onChange handler in dashboard.js (see the changed.has(...) block around line 1604). The layoutMode dropdown was dead UI for some time because nobody read graphSettings.layoutMode in computeLayout. Pattern to follow: when adding a setting that affects layout/render, add a changed.has(id) branch that calls renderGraph or whatever recomputation is needed.
insight.dashboard.dead-setting.summary: Settings in schema.js render UI and persist only — must be wired in dashboard.js onChange handler to take effect.

insight.dashboard.layout-not-wired.value: The layoutMode setting (radial/force/hierarchical) existed in schema but was never handled in handleSettingsChange in settings-palette.js — no changed.has(layoutMode) branch. Also computeLayout in layout.js always ran dagre regardless of mode. Fix: added layoutMode branch triggering full renderGraph re-run, and split computeLayout into 3 dispatched functions.
insight.dashboard.layout-not-wired.summary: layoutMode setting was not wired in handleSettingsChange and computeLayout ignored it — fixed both

insight.mcp-stdio-only-server.value: sharedMemory's mcp-server.mjs is stdio-only (JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout). It does NOT expose Streamable HTTP transport. The :3000 WebSocket is the project's own protocol, not MCP. To use Codex's "Streamable HTTP" tab (or any HTTP MCP client), you'd need to add an HTTP MCP transport using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk's StreamableHTTPServerTransport and mount it in src/server.js — ~30-60 lines plus tests. Not worth it unless remote/HTTP access is required; stdio works for all local clients (Claude Code, Codex).
insight.mcp-stdio-only-server.summary: sharedMemory MCP server is stdio-only; HTTP transport would need new code (StreamableHTTPServerTransport)

insight.mcp-vs-file-memory.value: Two separate memory systems in Claude Code: (1) File-based memory at ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ — auto-loaded via MEMORY.md index every conversation, personal to Claude. (2) MCP memory-mcp server — requires explicit tool calls or a hook to read; designed for multi-agent coordination. The hook bridges these: it reads MCP server and injects into Claude's context automatically.
insight.mcp-vs-file-memory.summary: File-based memory auto-loads; MCP memory needs hook or explicit call to reach Claude

insight.plugin-pluginDir-bug.value: Claude Code v2.1.138 throws 'Missing environment variables: pluginDir' when plugin.json uses ${pluginDir} in args or env. Workaround: use node --input-type=module -e 'import(process.env.INSTALL_DIR+"/file.mjs")' pattern so no ${pluginDir} reference needed. Also: directory and file marketplace source types are in the JSON schema but NOT supported at runtime — only github and git (with full URL) work.
insight.plugin-pluginDir-bug.summary: CC v2.1.138: ${pluginDir} in plugin.json args breaks; use node -e import() workaround

insight.public-static-exposes-everything.value: Express serves /public as a static directory. Found 4 Node.js patch scripts (fix-dashboard.js, fix-syntax.js, update-dashboard.js, update-click-handler.js) sitting in public/js/ — they used require(fs)/require(path) so they would throw in browsers, but anyone could GET /js/fix-dashboard.js and download what looks like dashboard internals. Deleted. Lesson: never put anything in public/ that is not intended for browser delivery — use scripts/ for one-shot tools.
insight.public-static-exposes-everything.summary: Anything in public/ is web-accessible — keep dev/patch scripts in scripts/ instead

insight.sharedMemory.audit-was-missing.value: {"description":"As of 2026-05-11, memory_audit, bulk_set, bulk_relate were documented in CLAUDE.md and in session.2026-05-06 memory but completely absent from code. Cause unknown (revert? stale branch? memory written before merge?). Re-implemented in same session as audit.","filesChanged":["src/protocol.js","src/memory-store.js","src/server.js","src/mcp-tools.js","mcp-server.mjs","test/server.test.js"]}
insight.sharedMemory.audit-was-missing.summary: audit/bulk ops were in docs+memory but not in code on 2026-05-11; cause unknown, re-implemented same day

insight.sharedMemory.cytoscape-focus-sta.value: {"summary": "Focus/hover/dim state managed via Cytoscape CSS classes (hover-main, related, dimmed, selected, updating). applyFocusState() runs BFS from focusedKey via focusDistances(), then assigns classes. Function-based styles like dimmedNodeOpacity() read graphSettings at render time. markNodeUpdating() uses cy.$id(key).addClass('updating') then removes after timeout.", "pattern": "cy.nodes().removeClass(...) then forEach to add classes based on distance map from focusDistances(focusedKey). Edges: highlight if either endpoint is in distances map, else dimmed."}
insight.sharedMemory.cytoscape-focus-sta.summary: Cytoscape focus state: class-based (hover-main/related/dimmed/selected) driven by BFS from focusedKey

insight.sharedMemory.cytoscape-svg-backg.value: {"summary": "To render rich content inside Cytoscape nodes without plugins: set background-image to a function returning a data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,<encodeURIComponent(svg)> URI. Use background-fit:'cover' and background-clip:'node'. Cytoscape re-evaluates function-based styles on cy.style().update(), so SVGs refresh when data changes.", "gotcha": "Each unique data URI is treated as a separate image load — but since data URIs need no network fetch, this is instant. Avoid caching issues by ensuring the URI changes when content changes (it will, since it encodes the content).", "pattern": "buildNodeSvg(key) reads currentEntries[key] and nodeIdentityColor(key), builds SVG string, returns encodeURIComponent data URI."}
insight.sharedMemory.cytoscape-svg-backg.summary: Cytoscape SVG node content: function-based background-image with data URIs, refreshes on cy.style().update()

insight.sharedMemory.welcome-auth-leak.value: {"description":"When MEMORY_TOKEN is set, the welcome message previously sent agentId to unauthenticated connections. Fixed 2026-05-11: welcome omits agentId when auth required; agentId is now included in the authenticated response instead. Clients that relied on welcome.agentId with a token-protected server need updating.","fix":"src/server.js line ~243: isAuthenticated ? {type:welcome,agentId} : {type:welcome}; authenticated response now includes agentId"}
insight.sharedMemory.welcome-auth-leak.summary: welcome message leaked agentId pre-auth; fixed by moving agentId to authenticated response when token auth is on

insight.sharedMemory.zombie-definition.value: A "zombie" memory entry is defined as one where any of: importance is 0, tags array is empty, or summary is empty/missing. The audit method excludes expired entries from the zombie list (they're reported separately in `expired`). This is enforced in memory-store.js audit() and mirrored in protocol.js auditMetadata() which produces ['missing-summary', 'missing-tags', 'missing-importance'] warnings on set.
insight.sharedMemory.zombie-definition.summary: Zombie = importance 0 OR no tags OR no summary. Expired entries are reported separately, not as zombies.

Capture decisions, facts, insights, project goals, blockers, action items, references, and preferences immediately when useful; search before writing; update existing keys rather than duplicating; use lowercase dot-separated namespaced keys; include summary/tags/importance; link new entries in the graph; update a daily session log; mention captures briefly. setup.claude-code-hook.value: File: scripts/memory-hook.js. Registered in .claude/settings.json under hooks.UserPromptSubmit. On every prompt: connects to ws://localhost:PORT, tries FTS5 search with user's query, falls back to top-10 by importance via export. Outputs hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext JSON. Requires memory server running (npm start). Timeout: 3s silent fail.
setup.claude-code-hook.summary: UserPromptSubmit hook that injects MCP memory into every Claude Code turn

setup.codex-mcp-stdio.value:
Codex CLI configured to share the sharedMemory MCP store with Claude Code via stdio. Added to c:\Users\pruth\.codex\config.toml:

[mcp_servers.sharedMemory]
command = "node"
args = ['D:\Pruthu\cv projects\test\sharedMemory\mcp-server.mjs']

[mcp_servers.sharedMemory.env]
MEMORY_FILE = 'D:\Pruthu\cv projects\test\sharedMemory\data\memory.db'

Uses the same mcp-server.mjs stdio adapter that Claude Code uses, so both clients hit the same SQLite store. No bearer token needed (stdio is local-only). Codex auto-discovers the 8 memory_* tools at handshake — no per-tool allowlist in config.toml. Restart Codex after editing config.toml for changes to take effect.
setup.codex-mcp-stdio.summary: Codex configured via stdio in ~/.codex/config.toml — shares same SQLite store as Claude Code, no token needed

setup.global-memory-directive.value: Global Claude Code config wires memory capture into every project: (1) ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md contains the always-on memory capture directive, (2) ~/.claude/skills/memory-capture/SKILL.md provides /memory-capture as an explicit sweep command, (3) ~/.claude/settings.json runs the UserPromptSubmit hook globally to inject relevant entries each turn. Full directive at D:\\Pruthu\\cv projects\\test\\sharedMemory\\prompts\\memory-capture.md. OpenAI agent adapter at examples/openai_agent.py supports both MCP stdio and WebSocket modes.
setup.global-memory-directive.summary: Global Claude Code: CLAUDE.md + memory-capture skill + UserPromptSubmit hook all wired

setup.memory-curator-agent.value: Project-scoped Claude Code subagent at .claude/agents/memory-curator.md. Audits the sharedMemory MCP store: missing summary/tags/importance, orphans (<2 graph relations), semantic duplicates, stale session.* (>30d) and completed task.* entries. Read-only by default; only mutates when the parent prompt explicitly authorizes (e.g. "merge the duplicates"). Tools: memory_search, memory_get, memory_map, memory_set, memory_export, Bash. Model: sonnet. First audit on 2026-05-05 returned a clean store (8 entries, all metadata present, min 3 edges per node).
setup.memory-curator-agent.summary: memory-curator subagent at .claude/agents/memory-curator.md — read-only audit of MCP store hygiene

setup.sharedMemory.plugin.value: Plugin: shared-memory@pruthuraj. Marketplace: github source pruthuraj/sharedMemory. Registered in known_marketplaces.json + extraKnownMarketplaces in settings.json. Plugin cache at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/pruthuraj/shared-memory/. plugin.json uses `node --input-type=module -e import(process.env.SHARED_MEMORY_INSTALL_DIR+'/mcp-server.mjs')` — no ${pluginDir} to avoid 'Missing environment variables: pluginDir' error in Claude Code v2.1.138. SHARED_MEMORY_INSTALL_DIR=C:\sharedMemory, MEMORY_FILE=C:\sharedMemory\data\memory.db, PORT=3000.
setup.sharedMemory.plugin.summary: Plugin registered as pruthuraj@github; uses node -e import() to avoid pluginDir env error

task.add-sri-dagre.value: public/index.html loads dagre@0.8.5 from cdn.jsdelivr.net without an integrity attribute. If the CDN is compromised the dashboard runs attacker JS. Compute the hash with: curl -s https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dagre@0.8.5/dist/dagre.min.js | openssl dgst -sha384 -binary | openssl base64 -A. Then add integrity=sha384-... crossorigin=anonymous to the script tag at index.html line 141.
task.add-sri-dagre.summary: Add SRI integrity hash to dagre CDN script in public/index.html line 141
